title: Thomsonaria ochracea
taxonomy: Thomsonaria ochracea belongs to the family Corallinaceae and is classified as a species of red algae. More specifically, it is a type of coralline algae, which is a group of marine red algae characterized by their ability to form calcareous structures. The classification of Thomsonaria ochracea is further refined to the genus Thomsonaria, which is a group of coralline algae that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species is often found growing on rocky substrates in shallow waters. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Thomsonaria ochracea.
